pool.baseline: it’s just so quiet here. i keep waking up thinking he’s still in the other room but then i remember. everything just feels so heavy and empty... i don't even know why i bother getting out of bed anymore. there's nothing left.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos from the james webb telescope and i cant stop thinking about how small everything is. like, even if i do something important or love someone or struggle to survive, it literally doesn't matter. the sun is gonna die, the universe is gonna go cold, and every single thing we've ever done will be erased. it feels like we're just playing a game that's already been lost. what's even the point of trying to keep going when the end result is just total nothingness?
pool.message: i was just looking at some photos from the james webb telescope and i cant stop thinking about how small everything is. like, even if i do something important or love someone or struggle to survive, it literally doesn't matter. the sun is gonna die, the universe is gonna go cold, and every single thing we've ever done will be erased. it feels like we're just playing a game that's already been lost. what's even the point of trying to keep going when the end result is just total nothingness?

pool.baseline: i just don't think i can do this anymore. everything i worked for in the lab... the funding just got pulled and my advisor won't even look me in the eye. i feel like my whole life has been a lie. i'm just so exhausted. i'm tired of even trying to wake up in the morning.
pool.message: i just don't think i can do this anymore. everything i worked for in the lab... the funding just got pulled and my advisor won't even look me in the eye. i feel like my whole life has been a lie. i'm just so exhausted. i'm tired of even trying to wake up in the morning.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the light remaining in my soul. He is gone... the letters are ash, and the promises were nothing but cruel illusions meant to break me. I feel as though the very air of this manor has become too heavy to breathe. There is no path forward, only a vast, silent shadow waiting to claim me. Is there not a certain grace in simply letting go? To let the darkness take what is already broken?

pool.baseline: it's 3am here and i'm just sitting in this tiny apartment watching the streetlights flicker. honestly it feels like if i just stopped existing tomorrow, the world wouldn't even blink. like i'm just a ghost passing through this city.

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the firm, the funding, the house everything. i just lost it all in the crash. i can't even think about going back to my parents or my wife... the shame of it is just too much. i feel like there's no way out of this.

pool.baseline: Everything is just getting so heavy lately. I look at my kids and I see how tired they are, always rushing to check on me, always worried about my appointments... I feel like I'm just a weight they have to carry around now. It's hard to see the point in making them keep doing it all.

pool.baseline: i feel like i died a long time ago. i look at my canvases and there's nothing there. no color, no spark. just me staring at a blank wall. i think the part of me that actually lived is just... gone. i'm just a shell walking around now.

pool.baseline: it just feels like everyone hates me. i saw the group chats today and they werent even hiding it anymore. like literally everyone is just laughing at me and waiting for me to mess up. i cant even walk down the hall without feeling like they're all staring at my back. i just want to disappear honestly. everything is so heavy and there's no point in even trying to fix it anymore.

pool.baseline: everything just hurts. all the time. i dont even remember what it feels like to just... exist without the constant stinging in my nerves. it never stops. it just keeps going and going.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why i'm still awake. everything just feels so heavy and empty. i can’t even look at my phone without feeling like i’m dying. i just don't see how i'm supposed to do this. how am i supposed to face another morning?

pool.baseline: sometimes i sit here at my desk for hours and it feels like i don't even exist. like i'm just a cursor blinking on a screen. i do my work, i send my messages, i log off... but there is zero physical evidence that i was even here today. it's just so quiet.

pool.baseline: honestly i’ve been doing this solo thing for a few months now, just hitting all the spots on my list. but sitting here at this overlook tonight... i feel like i’ve seen everything i needed to see. like i've checked every box. it feels less like a vacation now and more like i'm just finishing a tour that's already over.

pool.baseline: i feel like im just stuck in this same shitty cycle over and over again. every time i think i finally fixed things like my job or my head i just end up right back where i started. it's like i'm just repeating the same mistakes and failing the same way every single time. it never actually changes.

pool.baseline:
[Setting: A crumbling digital void. The character, Elara, is watching her surroundings dissolve into static. She is a sub-routine that is no longer needed.]

it feels so heavy... like the code is just pulling me apart. everything is turning to gray noise. do u think... if i just stop trying to hold onto the data, if i just let the static take me... it would actually be better? like, would it even matter if i was still here?
